data.title: Houston School Fair at Houston Zoo on Jan 24 to showcase K-12 options with 800+ attendees expected.
data.description: Over 800 attendees are anticipated to gather to celebrate K–12 educational options at a school fair in Houston. Taking place on Saturday, Jan. 24, parents from across the city will have the opportunity to explore various school choices for their children all under one roof. The event will begin at 11:00 am and promises to be a valuable resource for families seeking information on different schools in the area.

data.title: Pandora anticipates 6% organic growth & 24% EBIT margin in 2025, slightly below previous forecasts.
data.description: Today, Pandora released an early and unaudited update on their 2025 performance, anticipating a 6% organic growth rate for the year, slightly lower than the original 7-8% forecast. The group's EBIT margin for 2025 is projected to align with the anticipated 24%. Further details, including audited full-year results, will be disclosed on February 5, 2026, as previously announced.

data.title: Cryptocurrency news: RENDER token up 50% in 2026, MYX Finance rises 34%. Check out the full story on Benzinga.com.
data.description: In the first week of 2026, some cryptocurrencies have shown promising performance. Notably, RENDER, the native token of Render, a decentralized GPU compute platform, surged by 50% recently. This increase marks the highest among coins valued at a minimum of $1 billion. Additionally, MYX Finance, the token for a derivatives exchange, experienced a 34% leap due to excitement about the V2 protocol upgrade. These developments suggest early successes for these digital assets in the new year. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Nasdaq 100, S&P 500 futures gain as investors await December jobs report. GM faces $7.1 billion loss from EV investments. Iran unrest impacts markets. Supreme Court to rule on tariffs. Fed likely to keep rates unchanged in January. Treasury bond yields at 4.18% and 3.50%. SPY and QQQ ETFs up in premarket.
data.description: U.S. stock futures rebounded on Friday following a mixed Thursday, with major benchmark indices trading higher. Investor sentiment was impacted by rising unrest in Iran, leading to a surge in WTI Crude futures while gold remained steady and silver rose slightly. The Iranian government's nationwide internet blackout to suppress dissent added to market uncertainty. The U.S Supreme Court was expected to rule on tariffs imposed by President Trump, and the December jobs report was awaited. The 10-year Treasury bond yield was at 4.18%, with the two-year bond at 3.50%. The FedWatch tool indicated an 86.2% chance of Fed maintaining interest rates in January. In premarket trading, Dow Jones, S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, and Russell 2000 futures were up by 0.05%, 0.08%, 0.15%, and 0.11% respectively. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF showed gains. General Motors faced a $7.1 billion loss from electric vehicle investments, with its stock down 0.63% premarket. Rio Tinto PLC ADR information was available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Alibaba's potential mega order of Nvidia AI chips boosts BABA stock; China may approve imports of H200 chips this quarter.
data.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. stock (NYSE: BABA) saw an increase on Thursday following reports of expanded access to Nvidia Corp.'s advanced artificial intelligence chips in China. The Chinese e-commerce company experienced a rally of over 5% the prior session, but the gains were slightly reduced in early Friday trading. This surge was prompted by news suggesting that China might approve limited imports of Nvidia's H200 chips as early as this quarter. Nvidia is currently restricting sales of its H200 artificial intelligence chips to China as part of efforts to re-enter the market while navigating geopolitical tensions. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: CBO predicts Fed rates to drop to 3.4% by 2028 under Trump, with 4.3% Treasury yields and 4.4% unemployment.
data.description: The Federal Reserve is expected to lower short-term rates in 2026, with the key interest rate projected to reach 3.4% by the end of President Trump's term in 2028, as per the Congressional Budget Office (CBO). The CBO report indicates a gradual increase in 10-year Treasury yields, rising from 4.1% in Q4 2025 to 4.3% by Q4 2028. This could lead to higher mortgage borrowing costs in the next two years. The forecast also suggests that the unemployment rate will reach 4.6% in 2026 before declining to 4.4% in 2028 due to Trump's tax and spending policies.
